Introduction
Neuropeptide S (NPS, human sequence SFRNGVGTG-
MKKTSFQRAK)istheendogenousligandofthe7TMreceptor
NPSR.1 Via selective NPSR activation, NPS regulates several
biological functions including wakefulness,1,2 stress and
anxiety,1-5 locomotor activity,1-3,6 food intake,6-8 memory
processes,5,9 and drug abuse.10-13 To investigate these NPS
sensitive biological functions and to identify the therapeutic
potential of drugs interacting with the NPSR, potent and
selective ligands are required. To identify NPSR ligands,
structure-activity relationship (SAR) studies were performed
